title: Global Response to Hantavirus Outbreak on Cruise Ship
subtitle: Passengers from the MV Hondius return home as countries implement strict health monitoring protocols.

summary.en: A hantavirus outbreak on the cruise ship MV Hondius has led to a complex international effort to bring passengers home safely. Three people have died, and health authorities worldwide are managing the situation with careful medical checks and quarantine periods. While the virus is serious, experts emphasize that it does not spread as easily as other common illnesses. Different nations are using various methods, including hospital isolation and home monitoring, to keep the public safe. Health officials continue to track the situation closely to prevent further transmission.
summary.zh: 郵輪「宏迪斯號」爆發漢他病毒疫情，引發了一場複雜的國際行動，以確保乘客能安全返家。目前已有三人死亡，全球衛生當局正透過嚴謹的醫療檢查與隔離期來控管局勢。雖然該病毒相當嚴重，但專家強調其傳播力不如其他常見疾病。各國正採取包括醫院隔離與居家監控在內的多種方式來保障大眾安全。衛生官員將持續密切追蹤疫情，以防止病毒進一步傳播。

paragraphs.en: A major public health operation is currently underway as passengers and crew from the cruise ship MV Hondius return to their home countries. The ship became the center of a hantavirus outbreak, a rare illness usually spread by rodents. According to Deutsche Welle (DW) Top, the virus has caused three deaths, and health authorities are working hard to manage the health of everyone who was on board.
paragraphs.zh: 一項重大的公共衛生行動目前正在進行中，郵輪「宏迪斯號 (MV Hondius)」上的乘客與船員正陸續返回各自的國家。該船先前爆發了漢他病毒疫情，這是一種通常由齧齒類動物傳播的罕見疾病。根據德國之聲 (Deutsche Welle) 報導，該病毒已造成三人死亡，衛生當局正全力監控所有曾待在船上人員的健康狀況。

paragraphs.en: Reports from BBC Health indicate that the ship docked at a port in Tenerife, where passengers began the process of returning home. The situation began after a Dutch couple and a German woman passed away. While the virus is known to be rare, it can cause serious respiratory issues. Deutsche Welle (DW) Top noted that the specific strain found on the ship, the Andes strain, is one of the few that can spread from person to person, though it requires very close and long-term contact.
paragraphs.zh: 根據英國廣播公司 (BBC) 健康版報導，該船已停靠在特內里費島的一個港口，乘客們隨即展開返家程序。這起事件始於一對荷蘭夫婦與一名德國女性不幸過世。雖然已知該病毒相當罕見，但它可能引發嚴重的呼吸道問題。德國之聲指出，船上發現的特定病毒株為「安地斯病毒株」，是少數能人傳人的病毒株之一，不過傳播條件需要非常密切且長期的接觸。

paragraphs.en: Different countries are taking different approaches to keep their citizens and the public safe. BBC Health reports that in the United Kingdom, passengers were flown to a hospital for medical checks. They are required to stay for 72 hours and will then need to self-isolate—a period of staying away from others to prevent potential spread—for 42 days. Similarly, The Guardian Health explains that in Australia, passengers are being taken to specialized hospitals designed to treat serious infectious diseases. In Greece, one passenger faces a 45-day stay in a hospital isolation room.
paragraphs.zh: 各國正採取不同措施以確保國民與大眾的安全。BBC 健康版報導指出，在英國，乘客被送往醫院接受醫療檢查。他們必須留院觀察 72 小時，隨後還需進行 42 天的自我隔離，即透過與他人保持距離來防止潛在的病毒擴散。同樣地，《衛報》健康版說明，在澳洲，乘客被送往專門治療嚴重傳染病的醫院。在希臘，則有一名乘客面臨 45 天的醫院隔離期。

paragraphs.en: In the United States, the response has involved both government agencies and medical centers. According to BBC Health, American passengers are being screened at medical facilities in Nebraska and Atlanta. The United States Department of Health and Human Services has stated that the risk to the general public remains very low. MedPage Today reports that experts are watching the situation closely, noting that while the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) has been involved in the evacuation and monitoring process, the scale of the response has been a topic of discussion among public health professionals.
paragraphs.zh: 在美國，政府機構與醫療中心皆投入了應對工作。根據 BBC 健康版報導，美國籍乘客正在內布拉斯加州與亞特蘭大的醫療設施接受篩檢。美國衛生及公共服務部表示，對一般大眾造成的風險仍然極低。《MedPage Today》報導指出，專家正密切關注局勢，並提到儘管美國疾病管制與預防中心 (CDC) 已參與撤離與監控過程，但此次應對行動的規模已成為公共衛生專業人士討論的話題。

paragraphs.en: Experts are eager to reassure the public that this situation is not like the recent COVID-19 pandemic. According to Deutsche Welle (DW) Top, Professor Ann Rimoin from the UCLA Fielding School of Public Health explained that the Andes virus requires much closer contact to spread. She noted that while people may feel anxious, this is a very different virus and a different set of circumstances. Public health officials emphasize that the 42-day monitoring period is a precautionary measure—a step taken to prevent a problem before it happens—rather than a sign of immediate danger to the public.
paragraphs.zh: 專家們急於向大眾保證，此情況與近期的新冠肺炎疫情不同。根據德國之聲報導，加州大學洛杉磯分校菲爾丁公共衛生學院的安·里莫因 (Ann Rimoin) 教授解釋，安地斯病毒需要更密切的接觸才會傳播。她指出，雖然民眾可能會感到焦慮，但這是一種截然不同的病毒，且情況也完全不同。公共衛生官員強調，42 天的監控期是一種預防性措施，旨在防患未然，而非代表公眾面臨立即性的危險。

paragraphs.en: As the operation to bring everyone home concludes, the focus remains on careful monitoring. The Guardian Health reports that health agencies are using "active follow-up," which includes daily checks for symptoms like fever. Because the virus can have an incubation period—the time between catching a virus and showing symptoms—of six to eight weeks, this long period of observation is considered necessary. Authorities in countries like the Netherlands and France have also implemented their own versions of these safety rules, ensuring that every traveler is accounted for and monitored by medical professionals.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著將所有人送回國的行動進入尾聲，重點仍放在謹慎的監控上。《衛報》健康版報導，衛生機構正採取「主動追蹤」，包括每日檢查是否有發燒等症狀。由於該病毒的潛伏期（即感染病毒到出現症狀之間的時間）長達六至八週，因此這段長時間的觀察被認為是必要的。荷蘭與法國等國的當局也實施了各自版本的安全規範，確保每位旅客都受到醫療專業人員的追蹤與監控。

paragraphs.en: Ultimately, the international response highlights the challenges of managing health risks in a globalized world. While the outbreak on the MV Hondius has been a difficult experience for those on board, the coordinated efforts of governments and health organizations aim to ensure that the virus does not spread further. As passengers complete their isolation periods, health officials will continue to evaluate the effectiveness of these measures to better prepare for future health challenges.
paragraphs.zh: 歸根究柢，這場國際應對行動凸顯了在全球化世界中管理健康風險的挑戰。儘管宏迪斯號上的疫情對船上人員來說是一次艱難的經歷，但各國政府與衛生組織的協調努力，旨在確保病毒不會進一步擴散。隨著乘客陸續完成隔離期，衛生官員將持續評估這些措施的有效性，以便為未來的健康挑戰做好更充分的準備。

keyTerms.noteZh: 容易誤以為是動詞，其實是形容詞，常與 be 動詞連用。文中：A major public health operation is currently underway as passengers and crew from the cruise ship MV Hondius return to their home countries.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To know where someone is or to have confirmed that someone is safe.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 確認某人的下落或確保某人安全。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡不是「解釋」的意思，而是指確認人員行蹤。文中：Authorities in countries like the Netherlands and France have also implemented their own versions of these safety rules, ensuring that every traveler is accounted for and monitored by medical professionals.

keyTerms.definitionEn: The size, extent, or importance of something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「秤」或「刻度」，這裡指事件的規模大小。文中：MedPage Today reports that experts are watching the situation closely, noting that while the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) has been involved in the evacuation and monitoring process, the scale of the response has been a topic of discussion among public health professionals.

sources.title: Experts Wonder 'Where Is the CDC?' as a Hantavirus Outbreak Unfolds on a Cruise Ship

sources.name: Deutsche Welle (DW) Top
sources.title: Hantavirus: How is the outbreak being contained as passengers return?

quiz.question: According to the article, what is the specific requirement for passengers returning to the United Kingdom?

options.A: They must remain in a hospital for 45 days.
options.B: They are required to stay in a hospital for 72 hours followed by 42 days of self-isolation.
options.C: They are immediately released after being screened at an airport.
options.D: They must undergo daily medical checks for exactly eight weeks.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，英國的乘客被送往醫院進行醫療檢查，他們必須留院 72 小時，隨後需要進行 42 天的自我隔離。選項 A 是希臘乘客的情況；選項 C 與文章提到的嚴格監控程序不符；選項 D 雖然提到了監控，但 42 天而非八週才是規定的隔離期。

quiz.question: Why is the monitoring period for passengers set to 42 days?

options.A: Because the virus is guaranteed to kill anyone who has it within that timeframe.
options.B: Because it takes exactly 72 hours for the virus to become contagious.
options.C: Because the virus has an incubation period that can last up to six to eight weeks.
options.D: Because international laws require all cruise ship passengers to be isolated for this specific duration.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第六段提到病毒的潛伏期（incubation period）為六到八週，因此長期的觀察是必要的。選項 A 過於誇大，文中並未指該病毒必死；選項 B 混淆了住院時間與潛伏期；選項 D 將各國的防疫措施誤解為全球統一的法律規定，但文中並未提及此類國際法。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the word 'precautionary' mean as it is used in the context of the 42-day monitoring period?

options.A: Intended to prevent something unpleasant or dangerous from happening.
options.B: A punishment for people who traveled on a ship with an outbreak.
options.C: A temporary medical treatment used to cure an existing infection.
options.D: A sign that the person is definitely infected with the virus.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。文中提到這是「precautionary measure」，並解釋為「a step taken to prevent a problem before it happens」（在問題發生前所採取的預防措施）。選項 B 錯誤，隔離是防疫手段而非懲罰；選項 C 錯誤，這是一種監控而非治療；選項 D 錯誤，這只是為了預防，不代表乘客一定帶有病毒。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of this article regarding the MV Hondius outbreak?

options.A: The Andes virus is a global pandemic that is currently out of control.
options.B: Cruise ships are no longer safe and should be banned from international travel.
options.C: International health organizations are failing to coordinate their responses to the virus.
options.D: Governments are implementing coordinated, precautionary measures to manage the health risks of the cruise ship outbreak.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。文章整體重點在於描述各國政府如何採取協調的預防措施來應對 MV Hondius 號上的漢他病毒疫情。選項 A 與文中專家強調「這不是 COVID-19」的觀點衝突；選項 B 文章未提出禁止郵輪的論點；選項 C 與文章提到的「coordinated efforts」（協調努力）背道而馳。
